text:
Contarch (コンターチ, Kontāchi) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 131 Hunter × Hunter: The Stage 3 cast listings was a Greed Island player hired by Battera and one of ten founding members of an alliance formed to clear the game. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 132 

Appearance

Contarch has dark, afro-styled hair, a thin mustache, and prominent cheekbones. He wears a light, short-sleeved shirt overtop a dark, long-sleeved undershirt. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Contarch comes in contact with Mikli, Redwood, Pisac, Cuzco, Shihael, plus three other new Greed Island players, and convinces them to join the alliance. After Jispa guides the new recruits to the alliance's hideout, Contarch explains the spell-casting mechanics of Greed Island while another alliance member hands out a list of all the spell cards for the newcomers to study. Abengane is the first to voice that he has memorized the full list, prompting Contarch to quiz him on all of the defensive spells and their conversion limits, which Abengane answers correctly. Puhat then declares he is also ready, claiming he could take a harder question than Abengane, but fails to answer Contarch's question regarding the mechanics of the spell card "Compromise". Contarch shouts at Puhat to learn the list over. 

Several months later, Contarch, Nickes, and the other founders gather all of the alliance members to celebrate their progress. During the meeting, Genthru suddenly reveals himself as the Bomber and informs the alliance that he has planted bombs on all the members, demanding they hand over their specified slot cards, causing a massive panic within the alliance base. After Genthru explodes Jispa's face Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 142 and escapes with a "Leave" card, Contarch shouts that they should not try to pursue him, given that most of the alliance members joined on the basis that they lacked combat abilities. The alliance members continue arguing until Nickes pleads with everyone to comply and hand over the cards to save Jispa's life. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 143 

Contarch later joins a group of alliance members at the Starting Point while Puhat communicates with Genthru outside the game. Bara enters the game and presents Puhat's severed head to Contarch and the others, revealing himself as an ally of Genthru. He demands they send someone with the specified slot card data next time, as well as give him a "Leave" card. Assam objects due to the card's rarity, but Bara explains that he, Genthru, and Sub must be together to disarm the bombs; otherwise, the alliance members will all die if he cannot leave the game now. Contarch then quickly offers the card, though Bara punches him for not complying sooner. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 144 

Once Nickes finishes his meeting with Genthru and hands over the ring containing all of the alliance's specified slot card data, Genthru reenters the game alongside Bara and Sub. After confirming they were given all of the cards, Genthru, Bara, and Sub gather to "release" the planted bombs, though the "release" turns out to be a detonation and Contarch is killed along with nearly all of the alliance members. 

Abilities & Powers

Since most of the alliance members joined because they lacked combat abilities, it can be assumed that Contarch also had none. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Contarch was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Contarch's name is mentioned in the manga, and it can be inferred he is one of the founding members of the alliance, it is unclear which of the founders he is meant to be with the information given in the manga itself. However, the cast listings of Hunter × Hunter: The Stage 3, a stage play adaptation of the Greed Island arc, identify him as the member played by Hiroki Hirai. 

Anime and Manga Differences

Contarch's name is not mentioned in either anime adaptation.
In the credits of the 2011 anime adaptation, he is only listed as Man with Afro (アフロの男, Afuro no Otoko). Hunter × Hunter - Episode 64 (2011)
